insight: In base set openings, 'fastforge' (rushing Blastforge) is considered a disaster and should not be played. 'Fastimus' (rushing Animus) probably wins against 'Elyot Animus', but natural Conduit is the more solid play. P2 fastforge or fastimus is likely losing.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of base set opening theory and which early builds are viable or losing.

insight: In base set, the opening DD DD DDC GBD (double drone, double drone, drone-drone-conduit, gauss-blast-drone) is strictly worse than DD DD DDB SCD. The Conduit line is 'very significantly worse' according to expert analysis.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 was sharing and discussing a base set opening chart, critiquing specific opening lines.

insight: In base set, the line DD/DD DD/DA DDB/DTT is considered a difficult line that is close to a P2 win, but not easily so. By contrast, the DDA/DTT line is considered a P2 win 'basically on autopilot'. The DDB/DTT line is where autopilot sometimes makes small mistakes that can determine the outcome.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 and mwortel were discussing the difficulty and outcome of specific base set opening lines from a shared chart.

insight: In the DDB/DTT line, if P2 Rhinos the Steelsplitter, P1 can hold and buy another. But if P2 Rhinos the Gauss Cannon in that line, it is a significant mistake for P1 and they are in a losing position.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 was elaborating on why the Conduit variant of the DDB line is significantly worse, using specific Rhino targeting scenarios.

insight: Base set opening theory considers BSO (base set openings) to be P2-favored based on analysis of key turns 2, 3, and 4, though it has not been fully proven ('solved') by exhausting all possible options.
author: consensus
context: ruinedshadows and iminabearsuit discussed whether BSO is 'solved' and the meaning of that term in context.

insight: In base set, Conduit into Animus (CAB: Conduit-Animus-Blastforge) is an expensive opener because going Conduit-Animus leaves the player without Walls, forcing a costly three-tech build.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ was asking about base set Conduit plays and noted the constraint of CAB being expensive.

insight: Savior cannot be activated (clicked) until the player starts their turn with a resource of type A, B, or C already in play. P1 starts with a Savior and 3 Drones; P2 starts with a Savior and 4 Drones.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 was describing starting conditions for a game variant or brawl involving Saviors.

insight: In Prismata, only defender units have the 'prompt' ability (can act the turn they are purchased). There are no 'haste' units in the traditional sense; 'prompt' is the equivalent mechanic but is exclusive to defenders.
author: shadourow
context: A player asked about haste equivalents in Prismata, and shadourow clarified that only defenders have prompt.

insight: Master Bot is surprisingly easy to beat as P1 on base set. velizar_ reported not losing a single game as P1 against Master Bot, even when playing suboptimal openings like DD/DB.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ was discussing base set P1 play against Master Bot and noted consistent wins even with suboptimal lines, with amalloy sardonically agreeing it was 'unsurprisingly easy'.

insight: Master Bot responded to a P1 CDS opening by playing TR (Tarsier-Rhino), which velizar_ described as Master Bot 'throwing' the game, implying TR is a weak or losing response to CDS in that position.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ was testing a base set opening chart against Master Bot and observed MB play a poor response to the CDS line.

insight: Using the CDS (Conduit-Drone-Steelsplitter or similar) opening as P1 against Master Bot is an effective strategy; MB responded with TR which was exploitable, and velizar_ was using an opening chart to systematically exploit MB's weaknesses.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ reported using a community base set opening chart to consistently beat Master Bot as P1.

insight: Community members expressed interest in having more distinct AI opponents at slightly higher skill levels than existing bots, as a way to help players bridge the gap between Master Bot and multiplayer.
author: anesotericman
context: Discussion about improving the new player experience included suggestions for stronger intermediate bots as an alternative to immediately entering ranked queue.

insight: Learning that P1 T3 Animus and P2 T2 Animus are the default base set openings is considered foundational knowledge for new Prismata players, as it provides a baseline from which to understand how advanced sets deviate.
author: awaclus
context: awaclus endorsed teaching new players the default Animus opening timings as a core learning milestone.
